**Balatonberény** is a village in [Somogy](/wiki/Somogy_County "Somogy County") county, [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), with a popular nudist beach.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2) The country village had a population of 1,200 in 2011.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

The settlement is part of the [Balatonboglár wine region](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r_wine_region "Balatonboglár wine region").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### General history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: General history")]

A late [Bronze Age](/wiki/Bronze_Age "Bronze Age") cemetery has been found near the town.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

Many of inhabitants escaped to the reed-woods of Small Balaton (Kis-Balaton) close to the village and [Lake Balaton](/wiki/Lake_Balaton "Lake Balaton") when the [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") conquered the village.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

By 1733 the [Hunyady family](/wiki/Hunyady_family "Hunyady family") had become the largest landlord in the area.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

### Churches

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Churches")]

The town's first recorded church owned lands was noted in 1332. The [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic") church was built around 1350 and it got seriously damaged during the Ottoman invasion.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

### Castles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Castles")]

[The Star Castle](/w/index.php?title=The_Star_Castle&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Star Castle (page does not exist)") (known locally as the Csillagvár) lies at the border with the settlement of [Balatonszentgyörgy](/wiki/Balatonszentgy%C3%B6rgy "Balatonszentgyörgy"). The star-shaped, fortress-like building was constructed by [László Festetics](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A1szl%C3%B3_Festetics&action=edit&redlink=1 "László Festetics (page does not exist)") during the 1820s as a hunting lodge and an exhibition inside the building introduces the life of knights of border castles in the 16th century.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/aa/Balatonszentgy%C3%B6rgy_-_Csillagv%C3%A1r.jpg/250px-Balatonszentgy%C3%B6rgy_-_Csillagv%C3%A1r.jpg)](/wiki/File:Balatonszentgy%C3%B6rgy_-_Csillagv%C3%A1r.jpg)

Csillagvár - Balatonszentgyörgy.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Public bathing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Public bathing")]

In 1902 there were 170 bathing huts set up in the water, alongside each other across the beach-front as its popularity boomed during the turn of that century.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

The hot-water medicinal baths of [Hévíz](/wiki/H%C3%A9v%C3%ADz "Hévíz") and [Zalakaros](/wiki/Zalakaros "Zalakaros") can be visited by tourists.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The country village had a population of 1,200 in 2011.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

### Population change

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Population change")]

| Population | Year | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1,200 | 2011 | [[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2) |
| 1,155 | 2009 | [[5]](#cite_note-5) |
| 1,224 | 2007 | [[6]](#cite_note-balatonbereny1-6) |
| 1,210 | 2001 | [[6]](#cite_note-balatonbereny1-6) |

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/Map_of_Balaton.png/250px-Map_of_Balaton.png)](/wiki/File:Map_of_Balaton.png)

Balatonberény is next to Lake Balaton.

Balatonberény is next to [Lake Balaton](/wiki/Lake_Balaton "Lake Balaton"). The local hills are good for hiking and scenic experiences . A pleasant path goes to the [Bokrosi wine-hill](/w/index.php?title=Bokrosi_wine-hill&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bokrosi wine-hill (page does not exist)") that lies above the village, [Keszthely Mountain](/w/index.php?title=Keszthely_Mountain&action=edit&redlink=1 "Keszthely Mountain (page does not exist)") and the [monadnocks](/wiki/Monadnocks "Monadnocks") of [Tapolca hollow](/w/index.php?title=Tapolca_hollow&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tapolca hollow (page does not exist)") .[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Transport")]

There are 3 main roads, 1 bus-stop and a railway station in the village. The railway goes to [Budapest](/wiki/Budapest "Budapest") and [Balatonszentgyörgy](/wiki/Balatonszentgy%C3%B6rgy "Balatonszentgyörgy").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## The arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: The arts")]

The Transdanubia Amateur Art Festival is held in July.[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2) In summer exhibition of the fine arts in the Painters' House. Village Day is held on 1 May. .[[2]](#cite_note-hungary1-2)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonber%C3%A9ny&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Culture")]

The Hungarian folk song [Elhervadt cidrusfa](https://hu.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elhervadt_cidrusfa "hu:Elhervadt cidrusfa") was collected in Balatonberény in 1906 by [Béla Bartók](/wiki/B%C3%A9la_Bart%C3%B3k "Béla Bartók"). Balatonberény is also site of the Hungarian Emigration Memorial, situated at Balaton utca, opposite of the station.
